title: Dactylorhiza fasciculata
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza fasciculata is classified as a species of terrestrial orchid native to North America. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Dactylorhiza is a group of orchids that are commonly known as spotted orchids. Dactylorhiza fasciculata is further divided into two subspecies, Dactylorhiza fasciculata fasciculata and Dactylorhiza fasciculata brevicalcarata. This orchid is a member of the subsection Orchidion.
uses: Dactylorhiza fasciculata, also known as the clustered orchid, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Dactylorhiza fasciculata is also thought to possess antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for minor wounds and infections. However, its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential risks associated with Dactylorhiza fasciculata.
